findings.title: Real-time Co-Editing Feature Gap
findings.description: CollabDocs currently lacks real-time co-editing capabilities, which is a critical feature offered by Competitor X (LiveWrite) and Competitor Y (CoEdit Pro). This gap poses a risk of losing enterprise customers who are actively seeking this functionality.
assumptions: Enterprise customers prioritize real-time collaboration features.

evidence.excerpt: Competitor X launched live co-editing on 2025-10-15 with a heavy marketing push directly targeting our enterprise segment.
Three enterprise accounts have submitted formal feature requests for simultaneous multi-user editing.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of real-time co-editing features to achieve parity with competitors.

findings.title: Competitive Positioning of Workflow Features
findings.description: CollabDocs excels in document workflow features such as approval chains and version gating, which are not offered by competitors. This positions CollabDocs as a strong choice for organizations that prioritize governance and structured document management.
assumptions: Organizations value structured document management alongside collaboration.

evidence.excerpt: CollabDocs leads on workflow and governance features.

recommendations: Leverage workflow features in marketing to differentiate from competitors.

findings.title: Risk of Customer Churn Due to Feature Gaps
findings.description: The absence of real-time co-editing capabilities has led to formal churn conversations with enterprise customers, risking a combined ARR of $420K. Immediate action is required to address this gap to retain these accounts.
assumptions: Customers will churn if their feature requests are not met.

evidence.excerpt: Combined ARR at risk: $420K. Customer Success has escalated to Product with a 60-day window before formal churn conversations begin.

recommendations: Accelerate the development timeline for real-time co-editing features.

findings.title: Adopt CRDT or OT for Conflict Resolution
findings.description: As CollabDocs moves towards real-time co-editing, it is essential to implement a robust conflict resolution strategy. Evaluating both CRDT and OT approaches will be crucial to ensure a seamless user experience during concurrent edits.
assumptions: A well-defined conflict resolution strategy will enhance user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Engineering has evaluated two approaches: Operational Transformation (OT) and CRDTs.

recommendations: Conduct further analysis on the trade-offs of CRDT vs. OT for implementation.

summary: CollabDocs faces a critical gap in real-time co-editing features compared to competitors, which poses a risk of customer churn. However, the product's strengths in document workflow features provide a unique differentiation opportunity. Immediate action is recommended to close the feature gap and enhance conflict resolution strategies.
